what_i_noticed: Two signal quality gaps: (1) sp500_ytd_return was the last dead parameter — accepted by get_market_signal but never computed or passed by any of the 3 router endpoints (market, screener, stock signal). The momentum component only used 1M and 3M timeframes, missing the YTD trend. (2) The stock signal's PE-based valuation was extremely coarse — only adjusting for trailing PE >50 or <10, ignoring the rich information in forward PE vs trailing PE ratio. Stocks like NVDA (PE=38.5, fwd PE=17) showed no valuation signal despite having a 56% forward PE compression indicating massive earnings growth.
hypothesis: Wiring sp500_ytd_return into momentum and adding forward PE earnings growth signal will produce more differentiated and fundamentally-grounded buy/sell signals. YTD adds a third momentum timeframe that captures longer trends. Forward/trailing PE ratio detects earnings growth (bullish when forward << trailing) or decline (bearish when forward >> trailing) that pure price-based signals miss.
what_i_did: 1. Computed sp500_ytd_return in all 3 router endpoints (_compute_market_signal in stock.py, _stock_signal in stock.py, _compute_market_signal in market.py) by comparing current S&P 500 price to the last trading day of the previous year. 2. Blended YTD into momentum component: changed from 60% 1M + 40% 3M to 45% 1M + 35% 3M + 20% YTD. 3. Added forward_pe parameter to get_stock_signal. 4. Implemented earnings growth signal: forward_pe/trailing_pe ratio maps to [-0.15, +0.15] score adjustment. PE compression <0.6 triggers a bullish reason, >1.15 triggers a bearish reason. 5. Wired forward_pe from stock_analyzer's key_stats through both screener and stock_signal router endpoints.

after.pe_signal: graduated (trailing extremes + forward/trailing earnings growth ratio)

analysis: Both changes activate previously dead or underutilized code paths. The sp500_ytd_return fix eliminates the last dead parameter in the signal engine — all parameters accepted by get_market_signal are now computed and passed by all 3 router endpoints. The forward PE earnings growth signal adds meaningful differentiation: NVDA gets +0.15 (strong earnings growth), JPM gets +0.04 (moderate growth), while stocks with flat or declining earnings get neutral or negative adjustments. The data_generator's metrics (drift errors, backtest MAE) are unaffected because it doesn't test signal endpoints — the improvements are in the production /api/market-status/signal, /api/stock/screener, and /api/stock/{ticker}/signal endpoints. All 60 fast tests pass with no regressions.
next_steps: 1. Consider adding a sector-relative PE signal: compare a stock's PE to its sector median for more context-aware valuation. 2. GARCH-standardized residuals for block bootstrap (returns/conditional_vol) — suggested since cycle 4 but not yet implemented. 3. Add signal engine unit tests that verify the new momentum YTD component and forward PE earnings growth component produce expected outputs for known inputs. 4. Consider caching sector_momentum and forward_pe computations to reduce redundant yfinance calls.
